title: Mathematical Literacy
examination_body: Department of Basic Education, Republic of South Africa
level: Further Education and Training (FET) Phase - Grade 11
description: Mathematical Literacy at the FET phase equips learners with the ability to use mathematical concepts and processes to make sense of real-life situations. The subject focuses on applying mathematical techniques to solve problems within authentic contexts, promoting critical thinking and informed decision-making. It integrates mathematical skills with non-mathematical considerations to understand complex scenarios from everyday life, preparing learners for active participation in society.

topics.title: Representing Relationships
topics.description: Learners develop skills in representing various relationships using tables, graphs, and equations to model real-world scenarios.

topics.title: Comparing Relationships
topics.description: Learners compare and analyze multiple relationships, often involving different systems or options, to make informed decisions.

topics.title: Tariff Systems
topics.description: Learners investigate and interpret various tariff systems to understand cost structures and make optimal choices.

topics.title: Break-even Analysis
topics.description: Explains the concept of break-even point and applies it to business contexts to determine profitability.

topics.title: Interest
topics.description: Investigates simple and compound interest calculations, modelling their impact on loans and investments over time.

topics.title: Banking, Loans, and Investments
topics.description: Explores various banking products, loan scenarios, and investment options, evaluating their costs and benefits in real-world contexts.

topics.title: Inflation
topics.description: Examines the concept of inflation and calculates its effect on the price of goods and services over time.

topics.title: Taxation
topics.description: Covers personal income tax calculations, understanding pay slips, tax brackets, and the impact of salary changes on tax payments.

topics.title: Conversions
topics.description: Performs conversions within and between different measurement systems (length, mass, volume, temperature) using given formulae and tables.

topics.title: Length, Weight, Volume, and Temperature
topics.description: Applies accurate measurement techniques for length, weight, volume, and temperature, using appropriate instruments and interpreting values in context.

topics.title: Perimeter, Area, and Volume
topics.description: Calculates perimeter, area, and volume for various shapes and objects, applying formulae and interpreting plans in multi-step problems.

topics.title: Time
topics.description: Interprets and calculates time values, including using timetables and planning travel itineraries that consider various time-related factors.

topics.title: Scale
topics.description: Explains and applies the concept of scale to determine actual measurements from maps and plans, and to create scaled representations.

topics.title: Maps
topics.description: Interprets maps, identifies locations and routes, uses distance charts, and estimates travel distances and times for trip planning.

topics.title: Plans
topics.description: Interprets various types of plans (floor, elevation) to extract dimensions, identify features, and apply them in practical projects.

topics.title: Models
topics.description: Constructs and analyses models and 2D drawings, applying scale and evaluating designs for practical purposes like packaging and space utilization.

topics.title: Developing Questions and Collecting Data
topics.description: Learners develop effective survey questions and apply various methods to collect data from real-world contexts.

topics.title: Classifying and Organising Data
topics.description: Organises raw data into categories, frequency tables, and appropriate intervals to prepare for analysis and representation.

topics.title: Measuring Data
topics.description: Calculates measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) and spread (range) to describe and interpret data sets effectively.

topics.title: Representing Data
topics.description: Creates and interprets various graphs from given data, making estimations, deductions, and predictions about trends.

topics.title: Expressions of Probability
topics.description: Expresses probability in various forms (fraction, percentage, decimal) and understands related terminology like event and outcome.

topics.title: Evaluating Probabilistic Scenarios
topics.description: Conducts experiments to compare experimental and theoretical probabilities and applies probability concepts to analyse real-world data like motor vehicle fatalities.

topics.title: Probability and Decision Making
topics.description: Applies probability to analyse risk assessment profiles, evaluate the fairness of games, and critique probabilistic statements in media.
